note: awr_default/dwr_default collapse Bible 2.1-2.6's compound work-rate descriptors (e.g. Low-Med, Med-High) onto the schema's low|med|high vocabulary: Low->low, Low-Med->low, Med->med, Med-High->high, High->high. Six roles (flagged below) had no work-rate line in the Bible text; those defaults are inferred from the role's description, not sourced.

items.name: Sweeper Keeper
items.description: Starts high, acts as an eleventh outfield player in build-up, and defends the space behind a high defensive line.
items.archetype_note: Manuel Neuer (Bayern 19/20 season), Ederson. Not a licence: names are editorial reference points only.

items.name: Traditional Shot-Stopper
items.description: Stays on the line, commands the box, and distributes long and safe.

items.name: Ball-Playing / Stepping CB
items.description: Comfortable carrying the ball into midfield or hitting line-breaking passes, and can step into midfield to create an extra man in build-up when the opponent presses the pivot.
items.archetype_note: Gerard Piqué (prime Barcelona), John Stones (as the hybrid centre back and defensive midfielder).

items.name: Stopper
items.description: An aggressive, front-foot defender who steps out to kill attacks at source and wins his duels early.
items.archetype_note: Sergio Ramos (Mourinho's Madrid).

items.name: Covering CB / Sweeper
items.description: Reads danger, defends the space behind his partner, and wins footraces to balls played in behind.
items.archetype_note: Virgil van Dijk (also elite on the ball, roles can stack), Raphaël Varane (France 2018).

items.name: Wide CB (back 3)
items.description: The outside centre back in a back three who defends the channel and can step forward, or even overlap, in possession.

items.name: Overlapping / Attacking Fullback
items.description: Provides width high up the pitch, runs outside the winger, and delivers crosses and cutbacks.
items.archetype_note: Trent Alexander-Arnold and Andy Robertson (Liverpool), Dani Alves (prime Barcelona), Marcelo (Mourinho's Madrid).

items.name: Inverted Fullback
items.description: In possession, steps inside into central midfield next to the pivot instead of hugging the touchline, creating a central build-up shape, adding a body for counterpressing, and freeing the winger to stay wide.
items.archetype_note: Philipp Lahm (Guardiola's Bayern), João Cancelo, Joshua Kimmich (when at right back).

items.name: Defensive Fullback
items.description: Stays home, defends the flank, and keeps the back line intact.
items.archetype_note: Benjamin Pavard (France 2018, largely a defender first).

items.name: Wingback (back 3/5 systems)
items.description: Owns the entire flank alone, effectively a winger and fullback in one shirt.

items.name: Single Pivot / Deep-Lying Playmaker
items.description: The metronome at the base of midfield: receives from the centre backs, dictates tempo, breaks the first line, and screens the back four.
items.archetype_note: Sergio Busquets (Barcelona and Spain), Xabi Alonso (Mourinho's Madrid, with a longer, more vertical range).

items.name: Destroyer / Anchor
items.description: Wins the ball, breaks up play, and gives it simple: the insurance policy behind the rest of the team.
items.archetype_note: Casemiro.

items.name: Box-to-Box / Workhorse
items.description: The high-work-rate engine both ways: arrives in the opposition box, recovers into his own, wins duels, and carries through transition.
items.archetype_note: Jude Bellingham, Sami Khedira (Mourinho's Madrid), Leon Goretzka (Bayern 19/20).

items.name: Advanced 8 / Half-Space Creator (Mezzala)
items.description: An eight who drifts into the half-space between opposition lines to receive on the half-turn and create.
items.archetype_note: Andrés Iniesta, David Silva, Thiago (Bayern 19/20, a deeper variant).

items.name: Classic 10 / Playmaker Between the Lines
items.description: Lives in Zone 14, the central space just outside the box, receives between opposition midfield and defence, and plays the final pass.
items.archetype_note: Mesut Özil (Mourinho's Madrid, the transition-ten version), Antoine Griezmann (France 2018, a hybrid ten and second striker with high defensive contribution).

items.name: Touchline Winger
items.description: Stays glued to the touchline to stretch the pitch horizontally, receives to feet, beats the fullback on the outside, and crosses or cuts back.
items.archetype_note: Ángel Di María (Mourinho's Madrid), Kingsley Coman and Serge Gnabry (Bayern 19/20), prime Pedro (Barcelona).

items.name: Inside Forward / Inverted Winger
items.description: Starts wide on the opposite side to his strong foot, attacks the half-space diagonally, and shoots or plays the reverse pass, with the fullback behind him usually supplying the width.
items.archetype_note: Mohamed Salah and Sadio Mané (Liverpool), Cristiano Ronaldo (Mourinho's Madrid, the goal-scoring apex of the role).

items.name: Wide Forward / Wide Runner
items.description: A striker starting from a wide slot, less interested in receiving to feet and constantly attacking the space behind the fullback with diagonal runs in behind.
items.archetype_note: Kylian Mbappé (France 2018, the reference for the role).

items.name: Raumdeuter
items.description: A wide-listed player with no fixed station who drifts unmarked into scoring positions, reading where space will open and simply being there.
items.archetype_note: Thomas Müller (Bayern 19/20, an assist machine from nominally wide or ten positions).

items.name: Runner-in-Behind / Channel Runner
items.description: First thought is to run beyond the last defender: attacks the channels between centre back and fullback, stretches the pitch vertically, and finishes one touch.
items.archetype_note: Karim Benzema in transition phases (Mourinho's Madrid also used Higuaín this way), Fernando Torres (prime Spain, 2008 vintage).

items.name: Target Man
items.description: The reference point for direct play: wins the first ball aerially or holds it up with his back to goal, and brings runners into play.
items.archetype_note: Olivier Giroud (France 2018, zero goals but enormous function: pinning centre backs and laying off for others).

items.name: False 9
items.description: A striker who drops into midfield when his team has the ball, dragging centre backs into a dilemma between following him and holding their line.
items.archetype_note: Lionel Messi (Barcelona 2009-12, the defining example), Cesc Fàbregas (Spain, Euro 2012), Roberto Firmino (Liverpool, the pressing-first variant).

items.name: Complete Forward / All-Round 9
items.description: Can run in behind, hold up, finish, and link: broad, well-rounded attributes across the board.
items.archetype_note: Robert Lewandowski (Bayern 19/20, the finished article).

items.name: Poacher
items.description: Lives on the last shoulder and in the six-yard box: touches few, goals many.

items.name: Second Striker / Shadow Striker
items.description: Plays off the main nine, arriving late into the box from deeper: a goal-scoring ten.
items.archetype_note: Griezmann (France 2018 hybrid), Müller (interchangeable with Raumdeuter).
